metadata.procedure_phase: Stage 2 IN-PROGRESS at item 2.6 boundary. EA-4 has emitted v4 mid-stage handoff (commit d0c6df1) closing all of Item 2.4 (13 files), the f1 chokepoint helper fix, Item 2.5 (6 PS1 + 2 SKIP), and Item 2.5.v2 (AST-driven probe with 20/20 PASS). EA-5 has NOT YET been spawned. Guide-#6's first substantive task is to draft the EA-5 initiation pointer following the same pattern as Guide-#5 used for the EA-4 init pointer.
metadata.branch_at_handoff_blarai: chore/platform-extraction @ d0c6df1 (v4 mid-stage handoff commit; one parent ahead of 9d17e7f which was Guide-#4-to-5 handoff brief commit)
metadata.branch_at_handoff_devplatform: main @ 3894221 (UNCHANGED from instance 4 handoff; not re-verified this instance — Stage 2 is BlarAI-side only, devplatform untouched)
metadata.status_md_last_commit: aa09f6a (between-stages cleanup commit; UNCHANGED through instance 5 — STATUS append happens at Stage 2 close per Item 2.10)
metadata.fleet_pause_state: paused with original reason "platform separation v2 in progress" + last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved verbatim across EA-3 + EA-4 sessions; ZERO state.resume_fleet calls all of Stage 2.
metadata.worktree_state: 21 M + 2 D + 9 ?? = 32 entries. The 21 M are 4 inherited Stage 2 paths + 1 LA-side GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + 7 EA-3+EA-4 Python file refactors + 6 EA-4 PS1 modifications + tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (file 13). The 9 ?? include scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt + 2 EA completion reports + temp_for_responses/ (containing all Guide-#5 disposition XMLs) + tools/_project_context.py + tools/_vikunja_client.py + tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ + tools/tests/. All refactor + verification work UNCOMMITTED per single-commit-at-2.9 protocol.
metadata.pytest_baseline: 282 passed (HELD throughout EA-3 + EA-4 sessions; matches v3 baseline exactly; NET-ADD of +2 from f1 fix tests in test_vikunja_client_scope.py).
metadata.stash_zero: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical timestamp held since v3 emission; NEVER touched.
metadata.trigger_reason: la_request + stage_boundary_recommendation + self_detected_context_pressure (~65-70% consumed). LA explicitly chose Option B handoff timing: ratify v4 commit, then handoff BEFORE drafting EA-5 init pointer. Strategic preservation of Guide-#6 context for the meaty init pointer drafting + ongoing 2.6-2.10 reviews.

guide_handoff_brief.active_conversational_topic:
EA-4 just emitted commit d0c6df1 (the v4 mid-stage handoff XML, docs-only, +390/-0). I (Guide-#5) ratified v4 in the immediately prior chat turn. The LA's next steps per Option B plan: (1) LA saves THIS handoff brief as docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml (overwriting the prior instance-4-to-5 brief). (2) LA spawns fresh Guide-#6 chat with: GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + STATUS.md + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + Variant B chat-init. (3) Guide-#6 runs Variant B comprehension gate (re-reads source docs at HEAD). (4) Guide-#6 drafts EA-5 init pointer XML (same pattern as 2.4-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_4_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_FILE_7.xml). (5) LA spawns EA-5 with init pointer + standard attachment set. (6) Guide-#6 reviews EA-5 iteration-1 comprehension gate, then ongoing 2.6-2.10 work.
Guide-#6's IMMEDIATE conversational pickup will be drafting the EA-5 init pointer. The LA's "I have sent the Approval and instructions to the EA" referred to the v4-handoff-authorization handoff, which EA-4 has now executed.

decision.title: Multi-iteration disposition cycle for EA-4 file 13 closure + f1 + Item 2.5
decision.what: Reviewed EA-4's iteration-1 comprehension gate (APPROVED), then EA-4's file 13 completion (APPROVED with continue-recommendation into 2.4.v2 + 2.5 + 2.5.v2), then surfaced f1 finding (chokepoint guard accepted 0/negative) and authored Option (a) disposition (fix now in 2.4 closure with explicit LA approval), then reviewed f1 + Item 2.5 with 3 corrections required (g5-7 duplicate tests, g5-8 env-fallback omission, g5-9 McpConfigPath separate hardcoding), then re-verified corrections (APPROVED), then reviewed Item 2.5.v2 (APPROVED + v4 handoff authorization). Eight substantive review cycles total this instance.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#6 inherits this iteration history. Read all 6 Guide-#5 disposition XMLs in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ -- they encode the cumulative procedural decisions and the 4 g5-N acks (g5-1 through g5-4 carrying forward from the EA-4 init pointer + g5-5 through g5-10 introduced this iteration with various SATISFIED / FORWARD statuses).

decision.title: f1 chokepoint guard defect disposition (Option a -- fix now)
decision.what: EA-4 surfaced f1 finding via 2.4.v2 verification: tools/_vikunja_client._require_project_id accepts 0 and negative integers (Vikunja silently returns data for /projects/0/tasks). Three options: (a) fix now in 2.4 closure with explicit LA scope-expansion approval; (b) defer to 2.6; (c) surface to Guide-5 first. EA-4 recommended (b); I overrode with (a) on two grounds: (i) PATH CORRECTION -- g5-4 disposition concerns LEGACY tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py at 2.6, NOT the NEW chokepoint helper which is the f1 source; (ii) RULE APPLICABILITY -- the scope-expansion-stop-and-report rule (g4-2 / w14) targets ADDITIVE scope expansion, not defect closure in already-in-scope work. EA-4 applied the 2-line fix + 2 new tests; pytest confirmed 282 passed (was 280 → +2 NET-ADD per V matrix).
decision.successor_instruction: If similar in-scope-defect findings emerge during 2.6/2.7 work, apply the same disposition logic: distinguish ADDITIVE scope expansion (requires LA approval; prefer defer) from DEFECT CLOSURE in already-in-scope work (LA-authorized fix in current item is fine). Single Stage-2 commit at 2.9 absorbs everything regardless of chronological session organization.

decision.title: Item 2.5 PS1 corrections: 3 substantive issues EA-4's report did NOT flag
decision.what: EA-4's initial Item 2.5 report claimed "5 PS1 — added [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] above existing [string]$RepoRoot param. Zero internal churn; backward-compatible." Disk verification surfaced 3 substantive issues: g5-7 duplicate test definitions in test_vikunja_client_scope.py (lines 76-94 verbatim duplicates of 57-74; pytest silently overrode); g5-8 env-fallback default OMITTED from all 6 PS1 scripts (canonical signature mandates `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })` per Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 + iteration-2 g4-2 binding); g5-9 $McpConfigPath separately hardcoded in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1:19 + la_merge_approve.ps1:40 (per WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY G-1 fix #1 should derive from $RepoRoot via Join-Path). Issued APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS verdict; EA-4 applied all 3 corrections + caught one transient encoding regression (non-ASCII § character) on its own.
decision.successor_instruction: The LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16) means independent disk verification is non-negotiable -- EA reports DO miss things even when the EA is competent. Continue this discipline at every EA review. The 4 g5-N acks (g5-7/8/9/10) all SATISFIED at v4 emission per the v4 handoff binding_acknowledgments_27_total block (with g5-10 forward to 2.10 STATUS append). A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly entry queued for 2.10 STATUS per g5-10.

decision.title: 2.5.v2 verification probe accepted as high-quality engineering
decision.what: EA-4 authored scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 -- AST-driven PowerShell probe using [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile to extract ONLY the primary RepoRoot/BlarAIRoot ParameterAst.Extent.Text per script (preserving [Alias()] + [string] attributes + default expression), wrapping in a single-param scriptblock to ISOLATE env-fallback / alias / explicit precedence semantics from peer mandatory params (Role on wake_launcher with ValidateSet, Branch / TrackingTaskId / FleetReportsTaskId on la_merge_approve). Tests 4 axes (baseline / env-fallback / alias-binding / McpConfigPath-derivation for the 2 affected scripts). Achieves 20/20 PASS. Probe + evidence captured at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt; both inherit as ?? in EA-5 working tree.
decision.successor_instruction: If EA-5 needs to do similar functional verification at 2.7.v2 (V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests), the AST-driven probe pattern is a precedent worth referencing. Probe is re-runnable via `pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1` from BlarAI repo root.

decision.title: v4 mid-stage handoff emission timing (proactive at clean breakpoint)
decision.what: EA-4 self-assessed budget at ~85% after Item 2.5.v2 close and recommended v4 emission per i1-4 ("even slightly tight" trigger language). I authorized v4 with rationale: (1) cleaner breakpoint than mid-2.6 emergency-stop; (2) Item 2.6 has 4 substantive deliverables comparable to file 13 in complexity; (3) 282 + ALL_GREEN + invariants intact = ideal handoff baseline. EA-4 emitted v4 commit d0c6df1 (1 file, +390/-0, docs-only). Schema fidelity is excellent (matches v1/v2/v3 + adds V0a-V0k 11-check pickup verification + 5-entry decision log + 27-ack carryforward with status flagged).
decision.successor_instruction: If EA-5 hits similar context-exhaustion at any clean breakpoint before Item 2.9 single commit, authorize v5 emission immediately. The discipline is to avoid emergency partial-commit attempts; mid-stage docs-only handoff commits are the exempt pattern (per standing_rules_carried_forward in v4).

decision.title: Option B handoff timing (this Guide-#5 to Guide-#6)
decision.what: LA explicitly asked for strategic guidance on Guide handoff timing. Recommended Option B (handoff after v4 ratification, BEFORE drafting EA-5 init pointer) on context-preservation grounds: my v4 review is small + uses already-loaded context efficiently; EA-5 init pointer is a meaty ~250-line artifact best authored with fresh context budget; Guide-#6 inherits the heavy lift (init pointer drafting + EA-5 iteration-1 review + ongoing 2.6-2.10 work + possible v5 handoff or Stage 2 closure). LA agreed.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#6: your IMMEDIATE first substantive task (after Variant B comprehension gate) is to draft the EA-5 init pointer XML following the same pattern as 2.4-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_4_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_FILE_7.xml. Use that as your template; adapt for the Item 2.6 entry point + the cumulative iteration history. Naming convention: 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6.xml (or similar; align to the procedure-stage prefix convention in p14).

open_watchpoints.intro: All 15 watchpoints from instance 4's handoff (w1 through w15) carry forward unchanged in their underlying risk classes. Status updates per this instance:

watchpoint.description: Agents Cadence Monitor LogonType=S4U; Stage 4.7 Unregister + Register requires elevated PowerShell.
G-2 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory rewrite at Stage 4.7. Successor must surface to EA-5 if Stage 4 enters scope.
$BLARAI_PID preflight at item 2.8. EA-5 picks up this watchpoint at 2.8 execution. STOP and report if regex returns empty or non-3 per g4-5.

watchpoint.status_change: C4 + C6 satisfied at file 13 + 2.4.v2; C2 deferred to Stage 6
watchpoint.description: Deferred items: C2 still unscoped (full _vikunja_client.py impl beyond D.1 gate); C4 satisfied (vikunja_mcp/server.py file 13 §D.1 hardening done); C6 satisfied (extended grep planned at 2.6 deliverable 1).
S-1 / S-3 / A3-followup -> DevPlatform-Meta tickets at Stage 6.
Stale wake_launcher auto-stash at stash@{0}. DO NOT POP through any platform-separation stage. Byte-identical timestamp 20260424_013002 confirmed at v4 emission.
Optional Vikunja project rename ("BlarAI Agent Gates" -> "Agent Gates").

watchpoint.recheck_at: Item 2.6 EA-5 deliverables + every EA-5 review cycle
watchpoint.status_change: EA-3 + EA-4 work fully verified by Guide-#5; EA-5 deliverables are next
watchpoint.description: Multi-file disk-level verification on every EA report. Per dn14 + p16 (mature not minimal), Guide-#6 must continue independent disk verification of every claim EA-5 makes -- argparse flag declaration is necessary but not sufficient; verify resolve() call + load_config signature + actual file modifications via git status + diff stat. EA-4's reports were largely accurate but missed 3 issues at Item 2.5 that disk verification caught (g5-7/8/9). Trust + verify; never trust alone.

watchpoint.status_change: EA-3 v3 + EA-4 v4 both clean; pattern holds
watchpoint.description: Single-commit discipline (mid-stage handoff commits are docs-only). EA-5's only valid commits before Item 2.9 are: (a) v5+ mid-stage handoff XML if context exhaustion threatens (docs-only, single file in diff). The Stage 2.9 single commit folds all refactor + verification work. NO partial-commit refactor; NO emergency single-commit attempts.

critical_notify.ps1 + escalation_notify.ps1 inspection. EA-4 confirmed Appendix A.1 SKIP per direct file read (line 11 comment-example only). Both files NOT modified in git status. Pattern holds for any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope surfaces beyond canonical 7 (default = SKIP with Appendix A.1 citation; runtime-path discovery = STOP + report per g4-1).

Legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py dual-touch consistency. EA-5 must close at Item 2.6 deliverable 2 -- either (a) thin shim delegating to new chokepoint OR (b) full fold deleting LiveVikunjaClient + updating ~6 callers. v3 + v4 recommendation: thin shim now; full fold at Stage 5 cleanup. ESCALATE to Guide-#6 if 2.6 disposition leaves the file's load_config call site OPEN before Item 2.9.

**observation o1 handler-guards-vs-VikunjaClient routing structural decision.** Per my prior disposition observations: when the same MCP tool needs to call multiple Vikunja endpoints, where does the project_id guard live -- HANDLER level (explicit checks per handler, duplicated, easy to miss) or CHOKEPOINT level (single VikunjaClient._require_project_id, single point of enforcement)? Current implementation is CHOKEPOINT (B) at file 13. o1 is asking whether to also keep HANDLER guards (A) as defense-in-depth or rely on CHOKEPOINT alone. EA-5 must DECIDE and DOCUMENT rationale at Item 2.6 deliverable 4. Recommendation (defensible either way): rely on CHOKEPOINT alone -- single source of truth; redundant handler guards risk drift if the chokepoint contract evolves.

**D3 dashboard_maintainer.py:113 private-method abuse closure.** `live._request("POST", f"/tasks/{...}", json={...})` private-method call left in place per Item 2.4 deferral (D3 ratified). EA-5 closes at Item 2.6 by adding a public method to VikunjaClient (e.g., `update_task_dashboard_field(task_id, field, value)` or `raw_post(path, json)`) and routing this call site through it. NO more private-method calls allowed.

**g5-10 STATUS append required inclusions.** Per the v4 handoff and my prior dispositions, the Item 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry MUST include: A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly entry (test_async_post_gate.ps1 inverted alias direction; functionally equivalent per 2.5.v2 axis 3 PASS); g5-7/8/9/10 dispositions (g5-7/8/9 SATISFIED; g5-10 SATISFIED by this STATUS append itself); f1 disposition (chokepoint helper fix SATISFIED); encoding regression footnote (UTF-8 BOM transient; resolved); 2.5.v2 closure (20/20 PASS evidence pointer at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt). EA-5 enforces at 2.10.

guide_handoff_brief.anomalies_not_yet_in_status: None require pre-Stage-2-close logging. STATUS.md last touched at aa09f6a (between-stages cleanup commit, instance 2). The cumulative anomaly list for Stage 2 close (logged at Item 2.10 per g5-10 + watchpoint w18): - A11 (NEW): test_async_post_gate.ps1 inverted alias direction (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot vs the other 5 scripts' $RepoRoot-with-BlarAIRoot-alias). Functionally equivalent per 2.5.v2 axis 3 PASS. Stage 6 hardening can standardize if desired. - A12 (NEW, optional): 6 PS1 alias-vs-rename approach for the 5 scripts (engineering trade-off; EA-4 chose alias to minimize internal churn risk; spec mandated rename per Stage 2 XML 2.4.v2). Functionally adequate; documented as deviation. - A13 (NEW, optional): UTF-8 BOM encoding regression (caught + fixed by EA-4 in same iteration). Successor Guide-#6 + EA-5 will draft the final A11+ entries together at Item 2.10.

la_profile.intro: All 16 traits from instance 4's la_profile (p1-p16) carry forward. Re-confirmed during instance 5 work. Two new observations follow as p17-p18.
la_profile.trait: Non-dev "vibe coder". Re-confirmed throughout instance 5 -- LA relies on Guide for technical decision-making (e.g., f1 disposition Option a vs b), correction calibration (e.g., 3-correction surfacing at Item 2.5), strategic timing (e.g., handoff Option B).
Prefers XML-formatted Guide-to-EA directives. All 6 Guide-#5 dispositions emitted as XML files in temp_for_responses/.

**"Mature not minimal" motto.** STRONGLY re-confirmed throughout instance 5. The independent disk verification discipline (per dn14 + p16) caught 3 issues at Item 2.5 that EA-4's report did NOT flag. The LA's mandate for thoroughness is the procedural backbone.
Tables faster than prose. Maintained throughout instance 5 chat outputs.
Forensic analysis. Implicit in LA's continued endorsement of independent disk verification + grep checks + AST inspection.
Paste-attachment hazards. Not exercised this instance.
IDE focus reminders are context only. Honored.
One numbered list per response block. Maintained.
Welcomes proactive handoff recommendations at boundaries. LA initiated this handoff via the Option B discussion.
Spell out acronyms on first use. Maintained.

Guided expertise vs menu-of-options. Re-confirmed in f1 disposition (gave clear Option a recommendation with rationale, not menu) and Option B handoff timing (gave clear recommendation with comparison table).
Per-request file-write grants only. All Guide-#5 file writes were to temp_for_responses/ per the GUIDE_PROMPT v2.1 carve-out -- no separate per-request grants needed.
Two-file pattern (XML directive + Markdown brief). Single-file XML pattern was sufficient this instance for all Guide-to-EA dispositions.

**"temp_for_responses" folder convention** (lowercase, underscores -- the v2.1 GUIDE_PROMPT update). Files there are audit artifacts; DO NOT delete (per dn13). Old "Temp for responses" folder (capital T, spaces) is being phased out.
LA verifies XML output meticulously. Maintained -- no typos caught this instance, but the discipline of full ISO 8601 timestamps held.

**"Critical operation" + "mature not minimal" = thoroughness signal.** Re-confirmed throughout. Independent disk verification on EVERY EA report; never trust alone. The 3 Item-2.5 issues caught are direct evidence of this discipline's value.

**Strategic context-budget conversations.** LA initiated explicit Guide-handoff-timing conversation at the 2.5.v2 → v4-handoff boundary. They appreciated the tabular comparison of options (A/B/C/D) with my clear Option B recommendation + rationale. Pattern: when context-budget-affecting strategic decisions arise, give comparison + clear recommendation rather than asking LA to decide; LA prefers guided expertise (per p11 + this new instance).

**LA accepts Guide overrides on EA recommendations when justified.** EA-4 recommended f1 Option (b); I overrode to Option (a) with two-grounded justification (path correction + rule applicability). LA accepted without pushback. EA-4 also recommended different action choices that I sometimes endorsed and sometimes overrode (e.g., file 13 immediate continuation into 2.4.v2 endorsed; alias-vs-rename approach noted as documentable deviation rather than required correction). Pattern: Guide is the procedural authority; EA recommendations are inputs, not decisions. The LA defers to Guide reasoning when clearly articulated.

active_risk_ids.intro: Risk IDs that proved active during instance 5. Carryforward + status updates + new.
active_risk_ids.risk: Worktree contamination. Re-checked clean at every prior gate.
S4U elevation; CRITICAL for Stage 4.7. See w1.
resume_fleet vs unpause_fleet. EA-3 + EA-4 both honored verbatim ack n=2 throughout Stage 2.
Stage 4.7 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory. Watchpoint w2 -- carries to Stage 4.
$BLARAI_PID resolution. EA-5 invokes at Item 2.8; STOP+report on drift per g4-5.
VIKUNJA_PASS literal. Full closure at post-soak.
Vikunja label-ID drift. Registry seeded.
Multi-BlarAI-projects discovery. A8/A9 promoted at instance 2 cleanup; A10 deferred to Stage 6.
REST vs MCP for Vikunja access. vikunja_access_protocol block in Stages 2/3/4.
_project_context.py schema mismatch. Resolved at Stage 1 close + 1 cleanup.
$BLARAI_PID preflight regex. Same as F1.
Chokepoint guard accepted 0 + negatives. Closed by EA-4 + Guide-#5 Option (a) disposition + 2 NET-ADD tests. Now matches §D.1 spec.
tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py file 13 §D.1 hardening complete (list_tasks + search_tasks both keyword-only required project_id).
Extended grep at Item 2.6 deliverable 1 covers C6.
Stage 2 rollback acknowledged in every EA-3+EA-4 comprehension gate. Forward-binding.
Per dn14 + p16. See w12.
EA-3 v3 + EA-4 v4 both honored docs-only commit. See w13.
tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py disposition. See w15.
EA-4 confirmed Appendix A.1 SKIP per direct file read. See w14.
Compound labels with reference prefixes acceptable when anchor disambiguates. EA-3 + EA-4 both followed correctly.
Structural design decision at Item 2.6 deliverable 4. See w16.
live._request closure at Item 2.6 deliverable 3. See w17.
test_async_post_gate.ps1 inverted alias direction. Functionally adequate; documented anomaly. See w18 + g5-10.
g5-1/2/3/4 carryforward as principles; g5-5/6 satisfied at f1 disposition; g5-7/8/9 SATISFIED at Item 2.5 corrections; g5-10 FORWARD to Item 2.10 STATUS append.

source_doc_revisions.intro: Files touched during instance 5 lifetime. STATUS.md, all stage XMLs, all source-of-truth docs UNCHANGED. Six Guide-#5 disposition XMLs added to docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/.

revision.note: Independent verification of EA-3's 6-of-13 partial work. APPROVED. 4 g5-N acks (g5-1/2/3/4) introduced for next EA. Successor MUST read this file -- it establishes the ratification framework + dn14 / p16 disk-verification discipline.

EA-4 init pointer authored by Guide-#5. **Successor MUST read this file as the template for the EA-5 init pointer you will draft as your first substantive task.** Adapt for Item 2.6 entry point + cumulative iteration history (v1+v2+v3+v4 + all Guide-#5 dispositions).
EA-4 iteration-1 comprehension gate review. APPROVED. Pattern reference for reviewing EA-5's iteration-1 gate.
EA-4 file 13 closure + observation o1 + continue-recommendation. Successor inherits o1 watchpoint w16 + continue-recommendation framework.
f1 disposition Option (a) authorization. Successor inherits the disposition logic for in-scope-defect findings (additive scope vs defect closure distinction).
3-correction APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS for Item 2.5 (g5-7/8/9 + A11 deviation documented as g5-10). Pattern reference for catching EA-report omissions via disk verification.
3-correction re-verification APPROVED. Encoding regression catch noted as good defensive practice.
2.5.v2 APPROVED + v4 emission authorization. Probe methodology rated PASS_HIGH_QUALITY. Successor inherits the v4 handoff scope guidance for Stage 2 closure path.

revision.commits: not yet committed; LA action: save to docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml (overwriting predecessor instance-4-to-5 handoff) and commit BEFORE spawning successor Guide-#6
revision.note: You are reading this. Predecessor (instance 4 -> 5) handoff at GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml is recoverable from git history (commit 9d17e7f).
EA-4 v4 mid-stage handoff. Successor MUST read this file -- it is the authoritative work plan for EA-5 (Item 2.6 → 2.10).

revision.commits: folds into Stage 2.9 single commit per EA-4 disposition
revision.note: EA-4's AST-driven 2.5.v2 verification probe. Re-runnable. Methodology reference for EA-5's 2.7.v2 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring.

source_doc_revisions.revision_no_change: No changes to STATUS.md, any 0X_STAGEN_*.xml, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md, WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md, RECOVERY.md, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE.md, VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md, 00_MASTER_PLAN.md, GUIDE_PROMPT.xml, GUIDE_USAGE.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, devplatform/projects/registry.yaml, BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml. The 21 working-tree M files + 9 ?? entries enumerated in v4 ground_truth_at_handoff remain on disk uncommitted, awaiting fold into single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9.

first_action_for_successor.step: After your Variant B comprehension gate is complete, briefly acknowledge the handoff and flag any STATUS drift or git advance since 2026-04-25T22:55:00Z. Compare branch_at_handoff_blarai (chore/platform-extraction @ d0c6df1), status_md_last_commit (aa09f6a), worktree_state (32 entries: 21 M + 2 D + 9 ??) against actual repo state. Expected new commits since this handoff: only the LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml itself (this file).

**Read all 8 Guide-#5 disposition XMLs in addition to the Variant B mandatory reads:** (a) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml (b) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_4_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_FILE_7.xml (c) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_ITERATION_1_APPROVED_EA_4.xml (d) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_2.4_COMPLETE.xml (e) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A.xml (f) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml (g) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_AND_2.5_CORRECTIONS_APPROVED.xml (h) docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_2.5.v2_APPROVED_V4_HANDOFF_AUTHORIZED.xml Also read EA-4's v4 handoff (docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v4.xml) -- this is the authoritative work plan for EA-5. The (b) file (EA-4 init pointer) is your TEMPLATE for the EA-5 init pointer you will author next.

**Draft the EA-5 initiation pointer XML** following the same pattern as the EA-4 init pointer (file b above). Adapt for: - EA instance 5, predecessor EA instance 4, resume at Item 2.6 (NOT a file number; Item 2.6 has 4 deliverables). - Reading order: v4 (primary work plan) + your Guide-#6 ratification of v4 (when you write it as your standing handoff response) + Guide-#5 8 disposition XMLs (history) + standard set (Stage 2 XML, EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml, STATUS.md, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md, WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md, 00_MASTER_PLAN.md). - Required first actions: V0a-V0k pickup verification per v4's successor_pickup_verification block + emit iteration-1 comprehension gate. - 27 binding acks restated verbatim (with SATISFIED status flagged for g5-7/8/9/f1 + FORWARD for g5-10 + carryforward for the rest). - Item 2.6 4-deliverable execution order: extended grep first → o1 routing decision → g5-4 disposition → D3 closure (per v4 successor_immediate_next_actions step 4). - Stop conditions: $BLARAI_PID drift at 2.8 → STOP+report; legacy file open at 2.6 close → ESCALATE before 2.9; context exhaustion before 2.9 → emit v5 handoff (docs-only commit). Naming convention: align with p14 -- procedure-stage prefix. Suggested: 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6.xml

Provide the LA with: (a) the file path of the init pointer; (b) a short chat-paste message for the new EA-5 chat ("Execute the attached..."); (c) the attachment list (15+ files; same pattern as the EA-4 init pointer's attachment list, plus the v4 handoff and the 8 Guide-#5 disposition XMLs).

Wait for the LA to spawn EA-5 + paste back the EA-5 iteration-1 comprehension gate. Review per the GUIDE_PROMPT.xml ea_comprehension_gate_review procedure. APPROVED authorizes EA-5 to begin Item 2.6 deliverable 1 (extended grep). Then ongoing review of 2.6 through 2.10 work + possible v5 handoff if EA-5 hits its own context boundary.

prime_eA5_init_pointer_watchpoints.point: EA-5 init pointer must restate ALL 27 binding acks VERBATIM with status flags. Do NOT summarize or paraphrase. Successor EA-5 must restate them in iteration-1 gate.
EA-5 init pointer must enumerate Item 2.6 4 deliverables explicitly with execution order recommendation (extended grep → o1 → g5-4 → D3). NOT just point at v4.
EA-5 init pointer must include the V0a-V0k pickup verification block from v4 verbatim or by reference. EA-5's iteration-1 gate must report V0a-V0k results.

EA-5 init pointer must include explicit forward acks: g5-10 STATUS append at 2.10 + g5-4 closure escalation rule + observation o1 design-decision-required + D3 closure-required + A11 anomaly entry required.
EA-5 init pointer should reference the AST-driven probe at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 as a pattern reference for the V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring at 2.7.v2.

do_not_list.intro: Carry forward all 17 prohibitions from instance 4 (dn1-dn17). Add 3 new (dn18-dn20) for instance 5 work products + LA-emphasized "mature not minimal" mandate continuation.
do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT re-chase STATUS.md line 11 v2-banner HEAD staleness. Known chicken-and-egg.
Pre-execution invariant past-tense issue resolved at Stage 1 EA close.
Do NOT edit, or allow any Stage 1-5 EA to edit, BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md. V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred.
Do NOT confuse fleet pause with Vikunja server stop. Different concerns.

prohibition.status_change: ACTIVE -- Stage 2 in progress; HIGH risk; EA-5 picks up at 2.6

do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT relax acknowledgment-wait for Stage 2 or Stage 4 EA. Strict verbatim enforcement.
Do NOT debug a "physform-extraction" typo. Chat-only artifact in a prior EA summary.
Do NOT regenerate or revise Stage 0 / Stage 1 baseline JSON / exit reports. Frozen audit artifacts.

prohibition.status_change: ACCEPTABLE per LA-request trigger; v3 + v4 mid-stage handoffs precedent established

do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT emit a handoff after only a partial Stage UNLESS LA-requested OR per i1-4 trigger.
Do NOT modify this handoff brief or any predecessor handoff brief in git history. Permanent records.
Do NOT allow any Stage 1-5 EA to run `git stash pop` / `git stash apply` on stash@{0}.
Do NOT delete, modify, or "improve" GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_DIRECTIVE.xml or GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_BRIEF.md. Audit artifacts.

Do NOT assume LA's file-write permission to Guide carries forward across instances or even across topics within an instance. (Per the GUIDE_PROMPT v2.1 carve-out, temp_for_responses/ writes are pre-authorized for Guide responses; otherwise per-request grants only.)
Do NOT delete or "clean up" the Guide disposition files in temp_for_responses/. They are committed audit artifacts (or will be at Stage 2 close).

**Do NOT trust the EA's "I did X" reports without disk-level verification.** The 3 issues caught at Item 2.5 (g5-7/8/9) are direct evidence of why this discipline matters. EA-4 was competent, attentive, and surfaced its own f1 finding -- yet missed 3 things at Item 2.5. Successor MUST continue independent disk verification on every EA report.
**Do NOT accept a partial-commit Stage-2 mid-stage handoff.** Mid-stage handoff XMLs (v1/v2/v3/v4/v5+) are docs-only commits. Partial-commit refactor work is REJECT.

**Do NOT silently approve scope expansion.** Default disposition for any audit-surfaced PS1 (e.g., critical_notify) is no-action with Appendix A.1 citation. Scope expansion requires explicit LA approval after a discovered runtime BlarAI path. (Distinct from defect closure in already-in-scope work, which is LA-authorized fix in current item.)
**Do NOT shortcut analysis to save context window.** The LA explicitly chose "mature not minimal." Depth wins over speed.

**Do NOT confuse the legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py with the new chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py.** g5-4 disposition concerns the LEGACY file at Item 2.6 (fold-vs-shim decision). f1 fix concerned the NEW chokepoint helper. Path-confusion would cause significant procedural drift -- always cite full paths when discussing either file.

**Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial.** EA-4 initially recommended deferring f1 to 2.6; I overrode with Option (a) on the grounds that defect closure in already-in-scope work is distinct from additive scope expansion. The 1-line fix + 2 NET-ADD tests was the correct disposition. Apply the same logic to similar findings: distinguish ADDITIVE scope (defer; LA approval) from DEFECT CLOSURE (LA-authorized fix in current item).

**Do NOT skip the "mature not minimal" disk-verification step on EA reports that look clean.** EA-4's Item 2.5 report claimed clean implementation; disk verification surfaced 3 substantive issues (g5-7/8/9) the report didn't mention. The LA's mandate is non-negotiable. Independent verification on EVERY EA report; never trust report alone.
